description: Search Feature and Typography Improvements - Unified search for learned items plus lighter Japanese display font and heavier answer input

userStories.title: Add Noto Sans JP Light font to the project
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to add the Noto Sans JP Light font to the project so it can be used for Japanese text display.
acceptanceCriteria: Download Noto Sans JP Light (NotoSansJP-Light.ttf or .otf) and add to assets/fonts/ directory
Configure React Native to include the font (react-native.config.js or Info.plist/build.gradle)
Create a typography constant for the Japanese display font family
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
userStories.notes: Added NotoSansJP-VF.otf variable font. Created react-native.config.js for font linking. Created typography.ts with FONT_FAMILY_JAPANESE_DISPLAY constant and font weight helpers.

userStories.title: Apply lighter font to Japanese character display
userStories.description: As a user, I want the large Japanese characters in reviews and lessons to use a lighter font weight so they're easier to read.
acceptanceCriteria: Review screen character display (72pt) uses Noto Sans JP Light
Lesson card character display uses Noto Sans JP Light
Incorrect answer screen character display uses Noto Sans JP Light
Font renders correctly for kanji, hiragana, katakana, and radicals
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in simulator using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Applied TEXT_STYLES.japaneseDisplay (Noto Sans JP Light 300) to character display in ReviewSession.tsx and LessonCard.tsx. Both review and incorrect answer screens share the same characters style.

userStories.title: Increase answer input font weight
userStories.description: As a user, I want the text I type in the answer box to be slightly heavier so it's easier to read while typing.
acceptanceCriteria: Answer TextInput in ReviewSession uses fontWeight 600 (semi-bold)
Answer TextInput in LessonQuiz uses fontWeight 600 (semi-bold)
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in simulator using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added fontWeight: '600' to the input style in both ReviewSession.tsx and LessonQuiz.tsx StyleSheets.

userStories.title: Redesign Settings button to outline style
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Settings button to be more subtle and full-width so it doesn't dominate the home screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings button is full width (with standard horizontal margins)
Settings button uses outline style: transparent background, subtle gray border (~#888), dark text
Button has appropriate press state feedback
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in simulator using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Changed Settings button from solid purple to full-width outline style with theme-aware border (text.tertiary) and text (text.primary) colors. Added activeOpacity={0.6} for press feedback. Created reusable bottomButtons and outlineButton styles for upcoming Search button.

userStories.title: Add Search button to home screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Search button on the home screen so I can easily access the search feature.
acceptanceCriteria: Search button appears above the Settings button
Search button matches Settings button style (full-width, outline)
Appropriate spacing between Search and Settings buttons
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in simulator using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added Search button above Settings button using same outlineButton and bottomButtons styles. Spacing handled by gap: SPACING.md in bottomButtons. Added Search route to navigation types for TypeScript support.

userStories.title: Create SearchScreen with text input
userStories.description: As a user, I want a search screen where I can type to search for learned items.
acceptanceCriteria: New SearchScreen accessible via navigation from Search button
Search input field at the top with placeholder 'Search radicals, kanji, vocabulary...'
Search input auto-focuses when screen opens
Back button/gesture to return to home screen
Empty state message when no search query entered
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in simulator using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created SearchScreen component with auto-focusing TextInput and empty state message. Registered in RootNavigator. Navigation back is handled by stack navigator's default behavior.

userStories.title: Implement full-text search logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to implement the search logic to query learned subjects from the database.
acceptanceCriteria: Search queries subjects where started_at is not null (lesson completed)
Search matches against: characters, meanings (all in array), meaning_mnemonic, reading_mnemonic, readings (all in array)
Romaji input is converted to hiragana for reading matches
Results prioritized: meaning matches first, then mnemonic/explanation matches
Search is case-insensitive for English text
Returns subject type (radical/kanji/vocabulary/kana_vocabulary) with each result
Performance: search completes in under 500ms for typical queries
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
userStories.notes: Created searchSubjects function in database.ts with SQL JOIN on assignments (started_at IS NOT NULL) and LIKE matching for characters, meanings, readings, meaning_mnemonic, and reading_mnemonic. Accepts hiraganaQuery parameter for romaji-to-hiragana converted reading search. Returns results prioritized by match type (character > meaning > reading > mnemonic). All 10 new tests pass.

userStories.title: Display search results list
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see search results as I type so I can quickly find what I'm looking for.
acceptanceCriteria: Results displayed as a scrollable list below the search input
Each result shows: character (large), primary meaning, subject type badge
Subject type badge colored appropriately (blue for radical, pink for kanji, purple for vocabulary)
Results update as user types (debounced, ~300ms delay)
'No results found' message when search returns empty
Results count shown (e.g., '42 results')
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in simulator using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added debounced search (300ms) to SearchScreen. Results displayed in FlatList with character (using japaneseDisplay style), primary meaning, and colored type badge. Badge colors from SUBJECT_COLORS: blue (#00AAFF) for radicals, pink (#FF00AA) for kanji, purple (#AA00FF) for vocabulary. Shows results count and 'No results found' message.

userStories.title: Create RadicalDetailScreen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view radical details when I tap on a radical search result.
acceptanceCriteria: New RadicalDetailScreen accessible via navigation with subject_id parameter
Displays: character (or image for image-only radicals), all meanings, meaning mnemonic
Shows user's current SRS level badge for this item
Shows 'Used in Kanji' section listing kanji that use this radical (if available)
Back button/gesture to return to search results
Styled consistently with existing LessonCard component
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in simulator using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created RadicalDetailScreen with header (character/image + SRS badge), meanings section, meaning mnemonic, and 'Used in Kanji' section. Added getKanjiUsingRadical database function to find kanji that use a given radical via component_subject_ids. Added navigation routes for RadicalDetail, KanjiDetail, VocabularyDetail. Exported MnemonicText component. Added 6 unit tests for getKanjiUsingRadical.

userStories.title: Create KanjiDetailScreen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view kanji details when I tap on a kanji search result.
acceptanceCriteria: New KanjiDetailScreen accessible via navigation with subject_id parameter
Displays: character, all meanings, all readings (grouped by type: on'yomi, kun'yomi, nanori)
Shows user's current SRS level badge for this item
Shows meaning mnemonic and reading mnemonic
Shows component radicals with their meanings
Shows meaning hint and reading hint if available
Back button/gesture to return to search results
Styled consistently with existing LessonCard component
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in simulator using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create VocabularyDetailScreen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view vocabulary details when I tap on a vocabulary search result.
acceptanceCriteria: New VocabularyDetailScreen accessible via navigation with subject_id parameter
Displays: characters, all meanings, all readings
Shows user's current SRS level badge for this item
Shows meaning mnemonic and reading mnemonic
Shows component kanji with their meanings
Shows context sentences (Japanese + English)
Shows parts of speech
Audio playback button for pronunciation (if available)
Works for both vocabulary and kana_vocabulary types
Back button/gesture to return to search results
Styled consistently with existing LessonCard component
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in simulator using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Navigate from search results to detail screens
userStories.description: As a user, I want to tap a search result to view its full details.
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping a radical result navigates to RadicalDetailScreen
Tapping a kanji result navigates to KanjiDetailScreen
Tapping a vocabulary/kana_vocabulary result navigates to VocabularyDetailScreen
Navigation passes subject_id correctly
Back navigation returns to search screen with query preserved
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in simulator using dev-browser skill
